projects.name: webminal
projects.description: Practice Linux commands, write bash scripts, learn Python,C and more, right in your browser. No setup required.

projects.name: dduper
projects.short_description: Fast block-level out-of-band BTRFS deduplication tool.
projects.description: Fastest way to deduplicate files on BTRFS. Uses filesystem checksums instead of userspace hashing. Written in Rust and Python.

projects.name: ebpf-diskalert
projects.description: eBPF based tool to monitor disk usage, perform actions when threshold breached.

form.question: How to support
form.response: Whether you report bugs, send pull requests, help fellow community members on the forums or sponsor the project, every bit makes a real difference.

form.question: A small brief about your project
form.response: Webminal is a free, browser-based Linux terminal and learning platform running since 2011 on a single server with 8GB RAM. It provides real shell access (not a simulation) for students to learn Linux commands, system administration, and programming. Features include 38 hands-on tutorials, a Root Lab powered by User Mode Linux for practising LVM/RAID with full root access, live command tracking via eBPF, and a shared MySQL database for SQL practice. Used by students from Harvard, MIT, Stanford, Georgia Tech, Thapar, SRMIST, and 2,700+ institutions across 50+ countries.

form.question: One FOSS maintainer lesson for your younger self
form.response: Setup dedicated public forums for support, let community support each other whenever possible rather having dedicated admin email support id or chat.

form.question: Why do you do it? Why do you bother maintaining a FOSS project?
form.response: FOSS helped me when I was a student. Webminal is my way of giving back. I'll keep it running as long as it helps even one student.

form.question: If your repo had a theme song, what would it be?
Which file in your project would you most like to set on fire?
form.response: The backend isn't on fire, but it has accumulated plenty of dead code and commented-out blocks over the years that need cleaning up

form.question: What's your open-source villain origin story?
If you had to use one emoji to convey what it is like to be a FOSS maintainer, what would it be?
